slides.title: TrpB MetaDynamics — Week 6 Progress
slides.subtitle: SIGMA Floor Fix + Path CV Audit

slides.title: Last week: PATHMSD + fixed LAMBDA, Job 42679152 submitted
slides.subtitle: Where we left off at 2026-04-09
bullets: λ corrected 3.3910 → 379.77 nm⁻² (per-atom MSD convention, FP-022)
Switched FUNCPATHMSD → PATHMSD (source-compiled PLUMED 2.9.2 fixes FP-020 conda .so)
Job 42679152 submitted 2026-04-09: 50 ns single walker, ETA ~04-12
Carried action items: run → FES reconstruction → 10-walker
slides.footer: Slide 2 / 10  ·  Context recap  ·  see FP-020/022 in replication/validations/failure-patterns.md

slides.title: Job 42679152 ran cleanly — but walker stayed 100 % in O basin
slides.subtitle: Mechanically healthy, physically stuck: classic FP-024 signature

bullets.text: 50 ns completed · 25 000 Gaussians (PACE=1000 × dt=2 fs = 2 ps/Gaussian)
Total bias 48 kJ/mol · 0 NaN · no crashes
s(R) ∈ [1.00, 1.63] for the full 50 ns — walker never left O basin
Adaptive σ_s (HILLS col 4) collapsed to 0.011–0.072 — just 0.07–0.5 % of path axis
Diagnosis = FP-024 (adaptive-σ collapse under ADAPTIVE=GEOM without SIGMA_MIN)

slides.code:
# Re-derive s-range from COLVAR:
python3 -c "import numpy as np; d=np.loadtxt('COLVAR',comments='#'); \
  print(f's range: [{d[:,1].min():.3f}, {d[:,1].max():.3f}]')"
# Output: s range: [1.002, 1.631]

slides.title: Root cause — SIGMA=0.05 is a Cartesian nm scalar, not a CV-unit floor
slides.subtitle: PLUMED 2.9 METAD docs (primary source): "Sigma is one number that has distance units"
bullets: SIGMA is a single Cartesian length (nm); ADAPTIVE=GEOM re-projects it per CV
Without SIGMA_MIN, per-CV σ can shrink to ≈0 on slow axes (here: the s-axis)
25 000 needle-Gaussians pile at s≈1 → deep narrow well (48 kJ/mol bottom)
Gradient outside the well ≈ 0 → no net force on atoms → walker cannot leave O
4 parallel Explore agents (physics / literature / Longleaf forensics / PLUMED source) independently confirmed
sources: Branduardi et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2012, 8, 2247 — DOI 10.1021/ct3002464 (adaptive Gaussians)
slides.footer: Slide 4 / 10  ·  FP-024 root cause  ·  PLUMED 2.9 METAD docs + source inspection

slides.title: Fix — explicit per-CV SIGMA floor + ceiling (deployed 2026-04-15)
slides.subtitle: Production METAD line, md5 aca3f0c4… on Longleaf
slides.code:
metad: METAD ARG=path.sss,path.zzz \
    SIGMA=0.1 ADAPTIVE=GEOM \
    SIGMA_MIN=0.3,0.005   SIGMA_MAX=1.0,0.05 \
    HEIGHT=0.628 PACE=1000 BIASFACTOR=10 TEMP=350 FILE=HILLS
bullets: SIGMA: 0.05 → 0.1 nm (geometric seed, 2× wider)
SIGMA_MIN=[0.3, 0.005]  per-CV floor in CV units  (s ≈ 2% of path axis; z above thermal noise)
SIGMA_MAX=[1.0, 0.05]   per-CV ceiling to prevent over-smoothing of basin shape
10 ns probe (Job 43813633) submitted same day
FP-025: prior tutorial cited `[SI p.S3]` for SIGMA=0.2,0.1 — SI has no numerical SIGMA; citation was fabricated
slides.footer: Slide 5 / 10  ·  SIGMA floor fix  ·  see FP-024 / FP-025 in failure-patterns.md

slides.title: Probe: 10 ns, max s = 1.393 — stuck, or starting to escape?
slides.subtitle: s↔z correlation flips from -0.31 (orthogonal) to +0.49 (aligned escape) in last 2 ns
bullets: d(max s)/dt doubled: 8.6×10⁻⁵ → 1.9×10⁻⁴ ps⁻¹
Local bias at s≈1 reached 55.5 kJ/mol (≈19 kT; ~2× typical protein barrier)
Signal is consistent with barrier-crossing onset, NOT a confirmed escape (only 2 ns of data)
10 ns too short to reject either hypothesis → motivates 50 ns extension (Slide 9)
sources: Branduardi, Gervasio, Parrinello, J. Chem. Phys. 2007, 126, 054103 — DOI 10.1063/1.2432340 (original path CV s(R), z(R))
slides.footer: Slide 6 / 10  ·  Job 43813633 · see Drill_Prep 6D Q2 for "escape or noise?"

slides.title: Wrong turn — Codex adversarial review caught an SI re-interpretation error
slides.subtitle: FP-027: logged + systemic fix to prevent recurrence

leftBox.heading: My misread (3 h wasted)
leftBox.body:
SI p.S3: "λ = 2.3 / MSD = 80"
→ read "80" as λ = 80 nm⁻²
→ concluded we're 4.75× off
→ started alignment-method diagnostic

rightBox.heading: Correct reading (already in repo)
rightBox.body:
summary.txt L23-25:
"Reported MSD: ~80 Å² (interpreted as total SD)
Our total SD: 67.826 Å² (ratio 0.85×)"
→ SI "80" is total SD Å², not λ

bullets: Codex pushback (verbatim): "comparing different quantities, core premise unsound"
Logged as FP-027 + new rule 21: "SI numeric re-interpretation must read repo's existing notes first"
Systemic fixes: PARAMETER_PROVENANCE.md, Codex review gate for diagnostic pivots, transcript-as-prior
sources: replication/parameters/summary.txt lines 23-25 (repo's own reading of SI "80" as total SD Å²)
slides.footer: Slide 7 / 10  ·  FP-027 · see Drill_Prep 6E for "won't misread again" defense

slides.title: Offline CV audit — path CV is physically correct (4HPX projects to s=14.91)
slides.subtitle: Per Codex recommendation: project known structures onto PATHMSD with LAMBDA=379.77

bullets.text: Strongest evidence: Pf A-A (4HPX) → s=14.91 matches St closed (3CEP) → cross-species validation

bullets: All endpoints project correctly; z values all ≤ 0.025 nm² (structures ARE on the path)
Conclusion: CV physics is correct — problem is kinetic timescale, not CV pathology

slides.title: Job 44008381 — safe checkpoint restart, 10 → 50 ns (running now)
slides.subtitle: Two-step GROMACS+PLUMED restart protocol (Codex stop-hook caught 2 bugs → FP-026)
slides.code:
# Step 1: extend .tpr nsteps (can't override with -nsteps alone)
gmx convert-tpr -s metad.tpr -extend 40000 -o metad_ext.tpr

# Step 2: mdrun with -cpi; plumed_restart.dat has RESTART directive
gmx mdrun -s metad_ext.tpr -cpi metad.cpt \
          -deffnm metad -plumed plumed_restart.dat \
          -ntmpi 1 -ntomp 8
bullets: plumed_restart.dat = plumed.dat + RESTART directive (PLUMED 2.9 docs)
Runtime checks passed: HILLS append-only, no bck.*.HILLS
max s(R) = 3.494, peaked near 28 ns; walker since fluctuating in s ≈ 2.2–2.8
Fraction of frames with s > 3: 1.74 % (barrier probed, not stably crossed)

bullets.text: Decision rule (at 50 ns, or earlier if max s ≥ 5)

bullets: max s ≥ 5 → Phase 2 (10-walker SI protocol, 50–100 ns / walker)
3 ≤ max s < 5 → current band; let 50 ns finish
max s < 3  → Phase 3 (4HPX-seeded dual-walker, ≈1 day setup)
slides.footer: Slide 9 / 10  ·  Job 44008381 running on partition=hov  ·  FP-026 fixed in extend_to_50ns.sh v3

slides.title: Process improvements + next steps
slides.subtitle: Per-parameter provenance, hardened audit gates, private repo

leftColumn.heading: This week's process improvements
bullets: PARAMETER_PROVENANCE.md — every number has source tag (SI-quote / SI-derived / PLUMED-default / Our-choice / Legacy-bug) + verification status
failure-patterns.md: FP-001..FP-027 + 21 general rules (4 new FPs + 2 new rules this week)
project_structures.py CV audit is a hard gate: exit 0 / 2 / 3 / 4 with AuditError class; malformed input → exit 3 (not crash)
Repo privatized; 3 branches pushed to origin (fix/fp024-sigma-floor · feature/probe-extension-50ns · diag/cv-audit)

If Phase 3: build 4HPX-seeded Ain-PLP system (~1 day) → dual-seed multi-walker
